full_response:
STEP 1 :  Specify the space-time

We start with ordinary Minkowski space ℝ^4 endowed with

 ds² = −dt² + dx² + dy² + dz² ,  c = 1.

Now impose the identification                                  (1)
 (t , x , y , z)  ∼  (t , x + L , y , z) .

After quotienting by (1) the manifold becomes                ℳ = ℝ_t × S¹_x × ℝ²_{y,z} .

Locally the metric is still η_μν, but globally the x–direction is a circle of circumference L.
The Killing vector that generates the identification is

 K^μ = (∂/∂x)^μ  whose components in Alice’s coordinates are  K^μ = (0 , 1 , 0 , 0).

STEP 2 :  Alice’s frame

Alice sits at rest with respect to the compact direction (β_A = 0).  
Because the identification (1) is made at equal t, every point with the same t
but different x labels the same physical event; consequently Alice’s simultaneity
surfaces (t = const.) tile the whole manifold without overlap.
For her the topology is “space-like only’’ and nothing mixes with time.

STEP 3 :  What does Barbara see?

Let Barbara move with constant velocity v = β c in the +x direction relative to Alice.
Her coordinates (t′,x′,y′,z′) are related to Alice’s by the usual Lorentz boost

 t′ = γ(t − βx),  x′ = γ(x − βt),  γ = 1/√(1−β²).        (2)

Apply (2) to the identification vector n^μ = (Δt,Δx,0,0) = (0,L,0,0):

 n′^μ = Λ^μ_{ ν} n^ν = ( −βγL , γL , 0 , 0 ).                 (3)

Hence in Barbara’s coordinates the same physical point is reached by

 (t′,x′,y′,z′)  ∼  ( t′ − βγL ,  x′ + γL ,  y′ , z′ ).          (4)

The identification is no longer purely spatial; a shift in space is accompanied by
a shift in time Δt′ = −βγL.

STEP 4 :  Detecting the preferred frame

Because of (4) Barbara can perform purely local experiments that reveal she is not in the
special frame:

• Clock-transport experiment  
  Synchronise two identical clocks at one point.  Keep one clock at rest, carry the
  second once around the universe in the +x′ direction, then compare them.
  After the loop the travelling clock has moved by Δx′ = γL, hence through the identification
  it is also displaced in time by Δt′ = −βγL.  The two clocks disagree by

     Δτ = γ|Δt′| = βγL/c .                                     (5)

  If Δτ ≠ 0 the observer is not in the preferred frame; only Alice (β = 0) finds Δτ = 0.

• Two-way light-signal (Sagnac) experiment  
  Emit two light pulses simultaneously, one clockwise and one counter-clockwise
  round the S¹.  They reunite simultaneously only in Alice’s frame.
  Barbara measures a difference in arrival times Δt′ = 2βγL/c.

Thus an inertial observer can decide operationally whether she is at rest with respect to
the compact dimension—Alice’s frame is physically distinguished.

STEP 5 :  Why this does not contradict the “no preferred inertial frame’’ principle

The usual statement “no inertial frame is special in Minkowski space’’ assumes the
manifold is the simply-connected ℝ⁴.  There Lorentz transformations are global
isometries of both the geometry and the topology, so all inertial frames are equivalent.

Here the local geometry (η_μν) is still Lorentz-invariant, but we have imposed an
extra global condition, eq. (1).  That boundary condition is preserved only by the
sub-group of Lorentz transformations which leaves K^μ purely spatial—exactly the
β = 0 frames.  The topology therefore breaks the global symmetry and singles out
Alice’s class of inertial observers, even though locally the laws of physics remain
unchanged.

In short, the laws continue to respect special relativity locally, while the global
structure of space-time provides a detectable preferred frame.

\boxed{\text{Because the spatial identification (t,x,y,z)\sim(t,x+L,y,z) is made at equal%
\;t, it is purely spatial only in the inertial frame at rest with respect to the%
compact dimension (Alice’s frame).  After a Lorentz boost the identification mixes%
space and time, eq.\,(t',x')\sim(t'-\beta\gamma L,\;x'+\gamma L), so an observer who%
moves relative to Alice finds that clocks transported once round the universe disagree%
by \Delta\tau=\beta\gamma L/c or that oppositely directed light pulses return at%
different times.  Hence an observer can determine experimentally whether she is in the%
special frame.  This does not violate special relativity, because Lorentz invariance%
concerns local physics on the simply-connected Minkowski manifold, whereas the global%
topology (the periodic boundary condition) breaks the symmetry and selects a preferred%
set of inertial frames.} 

Confidence: 78%

\text{Because the spatial identification (t,x,y,z)\sim(t,x+L,y,z) is made at equal%
\;t, it is purely spatial only in the inertial frame at rest with respect to the%
compact dimension (Alice’s frame).  After a Lorentz boost the identification mixes%
space and time, eq.\,(t',x')\sim(t'-\beta\gamma L,\;x'+\gamma L), so an observer who%
moves relative to Alice finds that clocks transported once round the universe disagree%
by \Delta\tau=\beta\gamma L/c or that oppositely directed light pulses return at%
different times.  Hence an observer can determine experimentally whether she is in the%
special frame.  This does not violate special relativity, because Lorentz invariance%
concerns local physics on the simply-connected Minkowski manifold, whereas the global%
topology (the periodic boundary condition) breaks the symmetry and selects a preferred%
set of inertial frames.
